text: 23.9g of fat. polyunsaturated fat is where things start to fall off. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
40.8g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.
6.2g of protein. essential amino acids score is where things start to fall off. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 10% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 18% daily value, zinc at 10% daily value stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

on the plus side: holds up as a better snack default than most packaged options. that real-world swap value counts. fat and texture help satiety. they can hold up better than many snack foods. portable, practical snack food. nuts score real points in life because they are easy to use.

now the downsides: cost or allergy issues can limit use. practical downsides still count. good versions can get expensive. that affects how realistic frequent use is. calorie density is very high. that makes portion control a real issue.
C tier. Best only in small amounts when the calories stay under control.
